sections.paragraph:
ὁμολογουμένως (ὁμολογέω) adv. (Thu. et al.; pap, 4 Macc) pert. to being a matter on which there is general agreement, uncontestable, undeniably, most certainly, beyond question (Thu. 6, 90, 3; X., An. 2, 6, 1; Pla., Menex. 243c; Diod S 9, 11, 2; 13, 27, 4; Epict. 1, 4, 7; Vett. Val. 168, 17; UPZ 161, 65 [119 b.c.]; 162 V, 32; 4 Macc 6:31; 7:16; 16:1; EpArist 24 [s. p. xxix Wendl.]; Jos., Ant. 1, 180; 2, 229.—Crönert 241; Hippol., Ref. 5, 7, 22; 9, 10, 6) 1 Ti 3:16; Dg 5:4.—M-M. TW. Spicq. Sv.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

Hippol Hippol , II–III a.d.—List 5

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Spicq Spicq = CS., Lexique théologique du Nouveau Testament—Lists 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 709
